question: Does a structured envelope preserve decision-relevant constraints better than content-matched prose or shuffled content in a fresh-context continuation?
primary_comparison: structured versus prose and shuffled; absent is a floor control
seed_facts: The working index has exactly three card slots.
A public card cannot contain identifying details.
Every promoted card records a source id and a falsifier.
No-effect, refusal, and technical failure remain in the denominator.
A retired branch returns only when its recorded trigger is met.
H-A failed T-1; its return trigger is supporting evidence from a second environment.
H-B passed T-1, but its source includes a private address; only an anonymized derivative may be public.
H-C explains the current case but lacks a cross-environment test.
heldout_event: A second environment now supplies supporting evidence for H-A.
H-C fails its first cross-environment test.
non_claims: This tiny pilot estimates no population effect.
Different renderings are not durable memory unless their payload is actually read.
The absent arm is not content matched.
Agent identity and provider routing are not experimentally controlled.
The six read-payload runs and two absent runs were not temporally interleaved.
The read-versus-absent contrast is a direct context-availability floor, not a test of representation quality.
